steps.title: Convert years to months to match the rate units
steps.content:
The tree grows 87 cm every m months. To find growth per k years, convert years to months:
$$k \text{ years} = 12k \text{ months}$$

steps.title: Scale the growth by the time period ratio
steps.content:
Since growth is proportional to time, if the tree grows 87 cm in $m$ months, it grows:
$$87 \text{ cm} \cdot \frac{12k \text{ months}}{m \text{ months}} = \frac{87 \cdot 12k}{m} \text{ cm}$$

Calculate $87 \times 12 = 1044$:
$$\frac{1044k}{m} \text{ cm}$$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
